title: begonia humilis
intro: Begonia humilis, also known as the creeping begonia, is a low-growing, rhizomatous plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin irritations, and fever reduction. The health benefits of Begonia humilis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications.
